assertions.title: Rate limit in envío OTP frena reenvíos masivos

evidence.network: 5x POST /api/auth/email-otp/send-verification-otp with email test-rate@example.com -> all returned 200

assertions.issues: Rate limiting is not enforced on the OTP send endpoint. All 5 requests returned 200 instead of 429 after request 3. The backend uses Better Auth's global rate limit (100 requests / 10 seconds) but does not have a per-endpoint or per-email rate limit specifically for OTP sending.

assertions.title: Login staff/admin separado habilita APIs admin solo para rol autorizado

steps.action: Seed admin user via ADMIN_EMAIL=admin@simut.test ADMIN_PASSWORD=Admin123! bun run scripts/seed-admin.ts
steps.observed: User admin@simut.test already exists with admin role

assertions.issues: The seeded admin user exists in the database (confirmed via sqlite3 query) but sign-in fails with INVALID_EMAIL_OR_PASSWORD. The seed-admin script creates a user record with bcrypt-hashed password, but the hash may be corrupted or the password may not have been set correctly. Cannot establish admin session to test list-users access. This assertion is blocked pending resolution of the password authentication issue for seeded admin users.

assertions.title: Política CORS de auth con credenciales aplica solo a orígenes permitidos

steps.observed: 204 No Content (no CORS headers visible on preflight)

evidence.network: POST with Origin: http://evil.local -> HTTP 200 with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true; The disallowed origin 'http://evil.local' still receives credentials-enabled CORS headers

assertions.issues: The Hono cors() middleware is configured with origin: corsOrigin (from CORS_ORIGIN env var, default http://localhost:3000). However, when a request comes from a disallowed origin, Hono's cors middleware still responds and the CORS headers (including Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true) are still being sent. The correct behavior for Hono's cors with a disallowed origin is to deny the request or omit CORS headers entirely, but the response still includes Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true which is a security issue for credential-bearing cross-origin requests.

frictions.description: Admin user seeded but password authentication fails - seed script creates user but hash may be invalid
frictions.resolution: Could not resolve - password-based admin login is not working for seeded admin. Requires investigation of bcrypt hash in database vs expected hash.

blockers.description: Admin sign-in (/api/auth/sign-in/email) fails with INVALID_EMAIL_OR_PASSWORD even after seeding admin user with seed-admin.ts. Cannot establish admin session to test VAL-AUTH-010.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Re-ran seed-admin.ts, checked sqlite3 for user and account records, but password auth still fails. The seeded account uses bcrypt hash but the sign-in flow may not be using bcrypt correctly.

summary: Tested 3 assertions (VAL-AUTH-007, VAL-AUTH-010, VAL-AUTH-014): 2 failed (VAL-AUTH-007 rate limit not enforced, VAL-AUTH-014 CORS credentials leak to disallowed origins), 1 blocked (VAL-AUTH-010 admin login fails - password auth issue). VAL-AUTH-014 fails because Hono's cors middleware sends Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true even for disallowed origins. VAL-AUTH-007 fails because Better Auth's global rate limit (100/10s) is too permissive for the per-endpoint OTP rate limit requirement.
